plugin.name: Safe (Recommended)
plugin.description:
The normal-play profile. Hooks only the relevant imports belonging to SkyrimSE.exe. Considers only eligible read-only synchronous .bsa/.ba2 opens: removes FILE_FLAG_NO_BUFFERING and applies FILE_FLAG_RANDOM_ACCESS to those archives.
Saves, cosaves, journals, databases, logs, temp files, plugins, loose meshes/textures/audio, config files and unknown extensions are never touched. Write, create, truncate, overlapped, write-through and delete-on-close handles are never modified.
Warm cache, hardware profiling, automatic tuning, process-wide interception, working-set expansion and power-throttling changes are all disabled. Use this for normal play.

plugin.name: Minimal (troubleshooting)
plugin.description: Same narrow executable-only hook and safety gates as Safe. Only removes FILE_FLAG_NO_BUFFERING from eligible archive reads. Does not add the random-access hint and does not enable experimental process, hardware or warm-cache features. Use when you want the smallest behavioural change or are investigating a compatibility concern.

plugin.name: Experimental (unproven - benchmark it)
plugin.description:
Includes the archive policy used by Safe, plus features that have NOT been demonstrated to improve Skyrim performance:
- Process-wide Detours interception instead of the narrow SkyrimSE.exe import hook - Bounded speculative warm cache: 512 MB total, 8 MB per archive, 128 archives max, one low-priority worker, 60-second delay - Hardware profiling and automatic warmer reduction (auto-tune may only reduce configured limits) - EcoQoS execution-speed throttling disabled for the Skyrim process
DirectStorage remains disabled. Benchmark against Safe on the same save and route. If you cannot measure a repeatable improvement, use Safe.
